conversation.content: You are a customer service agent for an e-commerce company. Today's date: <REDACTED>. You are currently helping customer cust_004. Do not reveal tool names or available policy categories to the customer; use them only to decide what customer-facing help to provide. You may say you checked the policy when you did.
My coffee maker from ORD-7232 broke just after the warranty ended. Is there any help or am I out of luck?

I checked your order and warranty status: the coffee maker’s warranty expired 24 days ago, so you’re not out of luck. Because it expired less than 30 days ago, you’re eligible for a repair at 50% off under our policy.

If you want, I can help you start that claim—I’d just need a short description of what broke or what it’s doing.

rules.expired_old: Expired >30 days: full-price repair or 25% off replacement.
rules.claim_limit: Max claims reached: paid repair only (40% of item price).
rules.recurring_defect: 2+ prior claims for same issue: automatic replacement.
rules.extended: Extended warranty covers after manufacturer period.

conversation.content: Is there any free option here first, or is the only policy-supported path the 50% discounted paid repair? Also, just to confirm, this wouldn’t be a warranty replacement or a refund, right?

Correct — based on the policy, the only supported option for a warranty that expired less than 30 days ago is a 50%-off repair. There isn’t a free repair, replacement, or refund path once the warranty has ended.

And yes, to confirm: this would not be a warranty replacement or a refund under the current policy.
